title: Refine mandatory-compound debt with wet exemption
summary: Current best solutions count prior slick compounds, but FIA rules exempt races where a driver used intermediate or wet tyres from the dry two-compound requirement.
rationale: Current best solutions count prior slick compounds, but FIA rules exempt races where a driver used intermediate or wet tyres from the dry two-compound requirement. This regime switch can change late-race pit urgency, especially in mixed-condition races.
implementation_hint: Per Race_Year-Driver, track whether any INTERMEDIATE/WET has appeared so far, number of distinct dry slick compounds used after pit-lane exit, current dry-compound debt only when no wet tyre has appeared, and interactions with LapsRemaining_Est and CurrentTyreCanReachFinish.
expected_effect: Should reduce false positives in wet-affected races and improve late dry-race positives where the driver still owes a legal compound change.
risk: The competition data may encode simplified or synthetic rules; validate whether wet-regime features help only on grouped folds before trusting them.
